Ad Text:
Senior Investment Performance Analyst

12-Month Fixed Term Contract
Brisbane CBD, Flexible Working
Up to $180,000 + Super
The Company

A large financial services organisation is undertaking a significant performance uplift project and is seeking an experienced Senior Investment Performance Analyst to play a key role in strengthening investment reporting, analytics, and stakeholder insight capabilities.

This is a high-impact, 12-month fixed term contract suited to a technically strong investment professional who thrives at the intersection of performance analytics, portfolio construction, and executive-level reporting.

The Opportunity

You will support the investment team by enhancing and delivering robust performance reporting across multi-asset portfolios, while improving data integrity, analytics capability, and dashboard automation. The role sits at the center of investments, risk, compliance, and executive reporting, requiring both deep technical expertise and the ability to communicate complex insights clearly.

This position is critical to uplifting how performance, risk, attribution, and exposure analytics are delivered to internal and external stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities
Deliver accurate, timely investment performance and attribution reporting across diversified portfolios
Enhance performance analytics frameworks, including risk metrics, factor exposures, scenario analysis
Build and refine Power BI dashboards for investment, risk, and executive audiences
Support asset allocation analysis, exposure monitoring, and portfolio rebalancing insights
Conduct quantitative analysis including scenario modelling, stress testing, drawdown and risk analytics
Work closely with custodians and data providers to reconcile and validate portfolio data
Partner with stakeholders across Investments, Risk, Compliance and Finance
Contribute to process automation and continuous improvement initiatives within the performance reporting function
About You

We are targeting a profile with strong alignment to the following:

Proven experience in investment performance reporting
Background within superannuation, or organisations closely aligned to superannuation investments (asset managers, institutional funds, fintech investment platforms, investment consulting firms)
Strong multi-asset portfolio experience (equities, fixed income, alternatives, etc.)
Experience in asset management or institutional portfolio management environments
Advanced Power BI capability (dashboard development and data modelling essential)
Strong data handling skills (SQL, Excel; Python highly regarded)
Understanding of portfolio optimisation, risk models, factor analysis, VaR, stress testing
Ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders and explain methodology and data assumptions clearly
If this sounds like your next contract opportunity, please hit “APPLY” now.

For more information, contact Sam Bott on sam.bott@perigongroup.com.au

Are you an experienced Microsoft Certified Senior Data Engineer with expertise in cloud-based data solutions? We’re working with a company in the finance sector in Brisbane that is seeking a skilled professional with strong knowledge of Azure Data Factory to join their growing data team.

6 month contract initially - possibility of extension.

2 days in the office

Key Responsibilities:
•    Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Azure Data Factory (ADF)
•    Implement CI/CD pipelines and automation using Azure DevOps
•    Collaborate with data analysts, architects, and finance stakeholders to develop high-quality, reliable data solutions
•    Ensure data quality, performance, and security across all data integration processes
•    Contribute to cloud data architecture and integration strategies across the finance domain

What We're Looking For:
•    Proven experience with Azure Data Factory and Azure DevOps
•    Strong SQL skills and experience with ETL/ELT development
•    Experience with other Azure services such as Data Lake, Synapse, and Azure SQL
•    Background in finance or other regulated environments preferred
•    Understanding of cloud-based data architecture and modern data engineering practices
•    Strong problem-solving and collaboration skills


Bonus Skills:
•    Familiarity with data governance and compliance best practices
•    Experience with reporting/visualisation tools like Power BI
•    Knowledge of infrastructure-as-code and automation in Azure environments

Why Apply?
•    Be part of a large Data Modernization project in the finance sector - this will look good on your CV!
•    Work with modern Azure technologies in a collaborative team
•    Flexible working arrangements (Brisbane-based is preferred)



Please APPLY today, interviews next week!

Or for more information please text Anna-Maria on 0426 503 822 and I will get back to you.

Catholic Education in Western Australia (CEWA) is the second largest education provider in the State. We cater for more than 76,000 students in 162 schools.

CEWA strives to create progressive environments that enhance learning. Our culture is underpinned by our Vision, Quality Catholic Education (QCE) Elements and CECWA' Strategic Directions. Our aspiration is to build Catholic Schools of Excellence, underpinned by their evangelising Mission, in a system where all students, staff and parents are empowered as active members of their school and parish communities.





Senior Data Engineer

For over 150 years, our schools, colleges, early learning centres and out of school care programs, have been an integral part of education in Western Australia. Together we form the state's second-largest education provider, with more than 83,000 students relying on our nearly 14,000 dedicated staff each day. We are committed to fostering an environment where every child can achieve their full potential, and our office teams support schools to do exactly that.

The Role

The role of Senior Data Engineer belongs to the Application Development team in the Finance, Infrastructure and Digital Technology directorate. We have 5 office campuses across the state and this role will be based at our West Leederville office.

We are seeking 2 full-time ongoing Senior Data Engineers commencing in late March 2026.

The Senior Data Engineer is responsible for technical leadership of cloud data platforms, leading the design and build of strategic data products, as well as leading internal consultancy activities to enable business users and analysts to obtain valuable data insights.

The Primary accountabilities of this role include but and not limited to:

Provide leadership in designing, building and maintaining existing ETL frameworks, cloud data platform components, working with Azure SQL, Databricks and Azure Data Factory.
Lead the build and maintenance of strategic data products which conform to CEWA's business and non-functional requirements, using Power BI and other front-end technologies.
Ensure Human-Centered Design tools & skills are used to ensure effective UX and high-quality UI in data products, using Figma and other design tools.
Lead various internal consultancy services to business unit-based Business Intelligence Analyst, Business Analysts and other business users to facilitate data access, data literacy, understanding of technical tools and processes, to support business self-service.
Lead the design, implementation and maintenance of dimensional data models using Kimball methodologies (Star Schema) to ensure high performance and consistency for analytics products.
Perform complex data analysis and reporting using SQL, DAX, Python, Power BI and other technologies.
Ensure automated tests are implemented as part of all data product builds.
Provide leadership in utilising appropriate AI and ML tools and processes during the SDLC and within product features.
Ensure the team delivers a suitable level of documentation, including Data Definitions, Product & Process Descriptions, Support Wikis, etc.
Ensure a high Cybersecurity focus in all stages of the SDLC.

Benefits of the role

A competitive salary: Level L on the CEWA salary scale [$169,821 - $192,025 per annum] plus superannuation.
Additional paid leave during office closures over Christmas and Easter.
Salary packaging options in line with our not-for-profit status, including novated leasing.
Deferred salary program for eligible employees after two years of service.
Free onsite parking.
Corporate health insurance discounts.
Access to the Fitness Passport program.
Employee Assistance Program: access confidential counselling and wellbeing support for yourself and your family.
Ongoing professional learning opportunities, including a CEWA scholarship program to support further education.
Flexible work arrangements, where operationally appropriate.
Wellbeing initiatives including annual flu vaccinations and workplace health checks.
Parental leave provisions above statutory minimums.

Selection Criteria

A. ESSENTIAL:

Prepared to support the Catholic ethos in the performance of their duties.
Experience in a technical leadership role, in a collaborative and trust-based team environment.
7+ years of experience working as a Data Engineer or Business Intelligent Specialist, with Databricks or equivalent technologies.
7+ years developing Business Intelligence reports and dashboards in Power BI.
Strong programming skills and technical experience for data manipulation and management using PySpark, T/SQL and DAX or equivalent.
Strong understanding of data modelling patterns.
Strong UX and UI skills with proven ability to harness the full functionality of Power BI.
Experience optimising Azure/Power BI data solutions for performance.

Information for Applicants

To access a copy of the Position Description please click here: Position Description

Interested applicants should submit:

A cover letter briefly addressing the selection criteria.
A curriculum vitae outlining their employment history, qualifications and certifications and include a minimum of two professional references.

The successful applicant will need to provide:

National criminal history check as issued by the Department of Education WA.
100 points of identification

Applications for this role close no later than 5pm, Tuesday 17 March 2026. We reserve the right to close the advertisement early if a suitable candidate is identified. If you're interested in this role, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible to ensure consideration.

If you require support with submitting your application, please contact our Talent Advisors at careers@cewa.edu.au. Applications can be submitted by clicking apply and uploading your application to our website. Applications will not be accepted via email.

All employees of Catholic Education Western Australia are required to support the Catholic ethos in the performance of their duties. Catholic Education Western Australia also encourages applications from people of all cultural backgrounds, First Nations people, and individuals with disabilities or medical conditions who meet the requirements of the position. To be eligible to apply, you must be legally permitted to work in Australia.

Closing Date: 2pm Friday 6 March 2026 
face2face reference: 666489 
Locations: Canberra, Melbourne, Brisbane and Adelaide 
Contract Duration:  12 months + 2X12-month extension/s

Why Join?

Deliver high-quality system analysis across complex, integrated environments
Work on API and cloud-based solutions, including AWS and OAS integrations
Collaborate with skilled teams to drive innovation and technical excellence
Contribute to impactful initiatives that support business and community outcomes
Play a key role in producing system design artefacts to support development and testing
Grow your expertise in solution discovery, backlog readiness, and cloud analysis

About the Role

This role enhances system capabilities across integrated environments, translating requirements into clear designs, supporting solution discovery, and collaborating with developers, testers, DevOps, and architects to deliver robust, high-quality solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse and document business and technical requirements into clear system designs
Support API-based integrations, including JSON/XML, validation rules, and error handling
Assess cloud-based solutions in AWS for performance, security, scalability, and resilience
Produce and maintain functional specifications, process/data flows, and interface definitions
Collaborate with stakeholders to clarify requirements, resolve issues, and deliver solutions
Assist with backlog readiness and contribute to Definition of Ready outcomes

Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Systems Analyst delivering complex system analysis and design
Practical experience with API integrations, OpenAPI Specifications, and interface definitions
Ability to interpret source code, configuration, and logic to support issue resolution
Experience assessing cloud solution impacts in AWS or similar environments
Strong collaboration, analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills
Ability to produce high-quality system artefacts and support cross-functional delive

Australian Citizenship & Existing Baseline security clearance is a mandatory requirement for this position. 

If you have any questions or would like more details, don’t hesitate to reach out to Urvish Kahar at urvish.kahar@f2frecruitment.com.au.

Ad Text:
Your new role:

As the Senior Director - Health Funding Analysis, you will lead Metro South Health's strategic health funding and activity function, reporting directly to the Chief Finance and Sustainability Officer. You will provide executive leadership, expert advice and governance oversight to optimise activity-based funding (ABF) outcomes, strengthen funding integrity, and support financial sustainability. Within this role you will:

Provide enterprise-wide leadership on ABF, funding reforms, and State and Commonwealth policy, supporting service planning and financial strategy.
Analyse Weighted Activity Units, case-mix complexity, cost structures, and funding variances to drive performance improvement and support executive decision-making.
Advise on major projects, service redesign, and hospital expansion, modelling funding impacts and risks under multiple scenarios.
Assist in negotiations with the Department of Health on funding arrangements, activity targets, and performance issues, advocating for Metro South Health.
Represent the Health Service in state and national ABF forums, committees, and working groups, contributing to funding policy, classification, and costing standards.
Lead, develop, and sustain a multidisciplinary team of ABF analysts, Health Information Managers, and clinicians, building capability in funding intelligence and performance management.
About you:

You are a senior financial leader with a strategic mindset and a proven track record in managing complex funding, ABF, and performance frameworks. You excel at translating complex technical and analytical information into actionable insights and have strong experience guiding executive decision-making. Your leadership fosters collaboration, capability building, and accountability. In addition, you have:

Extensive experience in ABF, health funding strategy, financial governance, and performance management within a large, public sector organisation or multi-facility health service.
Proven ability to lead and inspire high-performing, multidisciplinary teams and manage complex stakeholder relationships.
Exceptional analytical, strategic, and communication skills, with the ability to influence and negotiate at an executive level.

For comprehensive information about this opportunity and what we are looking for in the ideal candidate, please view the attached role description.

What we can offer you:
We offer a family-friendly work environment that supports you in making the most of Queensland's laid-back lifestyle.
We are renowned for teaching and research excellence with strong relations with Queensland's leading tertiary institutions.
We want to empower you to build a long-term career, with countless opportunities to learn and grow, no matter your role
You'll have all the benefits of working for Queensland's world-class public health system including generous leave entitlements, attractive salary and remuneration packages, salary packaging and access to relevant professional development allowances and/or leave, study and research assistance, corporate discounts and employee wellness programs.
About us:

Our values were created by employees for employees to shape our culture and inspire positive interactions in the workplace. Diversity of ideas, skills, traditions, and customs is celebrated as one of our greatest strengths. Having a workforce that reflects and understands the needs and expectations of our community is important to delivering safe, kinder, and more inclusive care.

We recognise our strength comes from the diversity of our people and so we encourage people of all genders, ethnicities, ages, abilities, languages, sexual orientation, and family responsibilities to apply.

INTEGRITY | COMPASSION | ACCOUNTABILITY | RESPECT | ENGAGEMENT | EXCELLENCE

We are Metro South Health:

We are the major public healthcare provider for Brisbane's south side, Logan, Redlands, and the Scenic Rim operating five major hospitals and a range of community, specialty, and state-wide healthcare services.

We are bold, innovative, collaborative, inspiring. We save lives, change lives, and make the world a better place. We are part of and proud of the community we serve.

We are better together.

MinterEllison is one of Australia's largest independent law and consulting firms. With a heritage of almost 200 years, we are known for our excellence, and our authentic and enduring relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. Clients seek out MinterEllison to help them solve complex problems every day. We do this by our full-service legal offering and complementary consulting offering.

We offer opportunities to work on industry-leading mandates for top-tier clients, while being part of a high-performing and collaborative team that values excellence, diversity of thought, curiosity, and inclusion. We value our people and empower them to achieve their ambitions - with the support, trust and autonomy to grow their careers in meaningful ways.

At MinterEllison, we're leading the way with AI and other emerging technologies, powering innovation right across our firm. You will have the opportunity to engage with AI tools we've built, and other external AI tools, to enhance efficiencies and excellence internally and with our clients.

We support sustainable ways of working regarding how, when and where you work and offer a wide range of social, financial and health benefits (see https://www.minterellison.com/-/media/Minter-Ellison/Files/Careers/MinterEllison-Employee-Benefits-Dec_2025.pdf)

The Role

This role sits within the Business Insights and Solutions team in Financial Services and supports Finance-led initiatives through hands-on data analysis, validation and execution.

You will work across both project-based and operational activities, collaborating with Finance, Technology and Data teams to support data quality, system integration, testing and documentation. While reporting tools form part of the role, the primary focus is working with data across interconnected systems - understanding how it is created, transformed, controlled and consumed.

The role contributes practical delivery support to Finance transformation initiatives, including regulatory programs (such as AML), platform change and system refactoring. Success in this role is measured by delivery reliability, data integrity and knowledge continuity, rather than ownership of formal governance or strategic direction.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse and reconcile data across multiple enterprise systems using SQL, Python and Excel to investigate issues, validate outcomes and support Finance decisions.
Develop a strong understanding of finance platforms (including Elite) and how data structures support reporting and operational processes.
Work closely with Finance stakeholders to clarify and structure data and process requirements that enable effective solution design and delivery.
Provide data-driven impact analysis to support Finance initiatives, reviewing workflows and data flows to identify inefficiencies, control risks and upstream and downstream impacts across reporting, finance operations, internal controls and connected systems. This includes cross-system data mapping and integration work (e.g. expense management, data warehouses and other finance platforms).
Support major transformation initiatives, including regulatory programs (such as AML) and Elite on-prem to cloud migration, assisting with impact assessment, testing, reporting validation and staged or interim solutions to ensure data integrity and continuity.
Support system testing and UAT activities, including preparation of validation checks, defect follow-up and maintenance of clear documentation (decision logs, data notes and process descriptions), contributing to knowledge transfer and cross-team coordination across Finance, Technology and Data teams.
Contribute to reducing key-person risk by capturing system knowledge, data structures and operational decision history in a structured and reusable manner.

About You

Strong experience working with data across multiple systems within complex organisational environments, with the ability to investigate, reconcile and clearly explain impacts
Proven analytical and problem-solving skills, comfortable operating in ambiguity and evolving priorities
Experience supporting Finance or operational system initiatives, with awareness of data integrity, controls and compliance considerations
Strong SQL and advanced Excel capability; experience with scripting or automation tools (e.g. Python) is advantageous
Able to contribute to system improvement and refactoring discussions without acting as a Business Analyst or owning formal requirements
Strong documentation discipline and the ability to capture system knowledge clearly and accurately
Comfortable operating in an execution-focused role, contributing through hands-on analysis, coordination and follow-through

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Information Systems, Business, or a related field
Experience working in complex enterprise environments; professional services experience preferred
Experience working with enterprise finance platforms (e.g. Elite 3E), particularly within a legal or matter-based environment, strongly preferred given the complexity of systems and platform initiatives underway
Experience supporting system migration, integration or transformation initiatives is advantageous
Experience with reporting or visualisation tools (including Power BI) useful, but this role is not primarily focused on dashboard development





How to apply

We encourage applications from people of all ages, abilities, cultural backgrounds, genders (including trans or gender diverse), LGBTQ+ people and those with carer responsibilities. We particularly encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people to apply.

We prefer to connect with people directly, so please submit your CV by clicking on the 'Apply' button. We encourage all applications, including if you do not meet all the criteria listed for the role. Your application will also enable us to consider you for other opportunities that may be available at MinterEllison.

If you are currently a MinterEllison employee, please apply through the internal careers page.

If you would like further information, require any adjustments throughout the recruitment process or for a confidential discussion, please contact Danielle.Miller@minterellison.com.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Join one of Australia's leading national retailers as an AI/ML Platform Engineering Manager, where you will play a pivotal role in shaping and scaling the organisation's next-generation machine learning capabilities. This is a permanent leadership opportunity overseeing a high-performing team of six engineers and driving end-to-end ownership of the AI/ML platform.

About the Role

In this role, you will lead the design, development, and delivery of critical AI/ML platform components supporting enterprise-wide machine learning initiatives. You will partner closely with data scientists, ML engineers, software developers, and platform teams to translate business needs into scalable and secure platform capabilities.

Key Responsibilities


Lead the architecture and development of AI/ML platform components including model training environments, feature stores, model registries, deployment pipelines, and monitoring frameworks.
Manage end-to-end delivery of platform enhancements, ensuring scalability, reliability, and high performance across all ML workloads.
Collaborate cross-functionally with technical and non-technical stakeholders to gather requirements and enable seamless delivery of AI/ML solutions.
Implement and champion best practices in MLOps, including model versioning, lifecycle management, CI/CD, observability, and platform security.
Drive continuous improvement by integrating new tools, frameworks, and automation to streamline and accelerate ML workflows.
Oversee platform reliability, incident response, operational support, and capacity planning to maintain enterprise-grade availability.
Ensure all platform capabilities adhere to organisational data governance, security standards, and relevant regulatory requirements.
Provide regular reporting, insights, and strategic recommendations to senior leadership on platform performance, risks, and future opportunities.


About You

You are a technical leader with a passion for building robust, scalable AI/ML platforms and empowering teams to deliver impactful machine learning outcomes. You thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and bring deep experience in MLOps, automation, and cloud-native engineering.

What's on Offer


Permanent leadership role in a high-growth AI/ML environment
Opportunity to shape a major retailer's enterprise ML ecosystem
Lead and develop a team of six talented engineers
Competitive salary, benefits, and long-term career pathways

For more information contact Melissa Haddad at melissa.haddad@talentinternational.com

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Transformation team as a Portfolio Reporting Analyst.  If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane, we’d love you to apply. 

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As a Portfolio Reporting Analyst, you’ll play a key role in enabling this transformation by delivering high‑quality reporting and insights across our Transformation portfolio.

This role is responsible for developing and maintaining dashboards and reports that provide clear visibility of performance, progress, risks and benefits across major transformation initiatives. Your work will support informed, data‑driven decision‑making at all levels, including executive and board forums.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Design, build and maintain dashboards and reports that track KPIs, milestones, risks and benefits realisation across the portfolio
Extract, transform and analyse data from multiple systems to meet reporting requirements
Partner with project teams and stakeholders to understand reporting needs and deliver tailored, actionable insights
Ensure data accuracy, consistency and integrity across all reporting outputs
Support automation and continuous improvement of reporting processes to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort
Maintain clear documentation of reporting processes, data sources and definitions
Provide insights and recommendations that support strategic decision‑making
Prepare reports for regulators and governance forums, including executive and board‑level committees
Ensure reporting complies with ART’s data governance and security standards

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Experience in reporting, data analysis or business intelligence within a project or transformation environment
Strong capability with reporting and data visualisation tools such as Power BI, Tableau and Excel
Proven analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and present insights clearly
Experience working with project management tools and data sources (e.g. Jira, Smartsheet)
Tertiary qualifications in Business, Information Systems, Data Analytics or a related discipline
High attention to detail and a strong commitment to data quality

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Sunday, 8th March 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As a Portfolio Governance Analyst, you’ll play a critical role in shaping how our programs and projects are governed, reported and assured across the enterprise.

This role is responsible for establishing, maintaining and continuously improving ART’s portfolio governance framework across large‑scale, complex and regulated programs. Acting as a trusted advisor to delivery teams and senior leaders, you’ll enable high‑quality decision‑making through strong governance, clear reporting and effective risk and control practices.

You’ll work across both traditional and agile delivery environments, ensuring governance approaches are fit‑for‑purpose, scalable and aligned to ART’s organisational policies and internal controls.

 

Day to day, you'll:

Own and maintain ART’s delivery and portfolio governance framework, including standards, policies, tools, templates and training materials
Provide clear guidance to delivery teams on applying governance across predictive, iterative and agile delivery approaches
Partner closely with Enterprise Portfolio Management, Technology, Risk and Finance teams to ensure governance alignment across the organisation
Monitor portfolio performance against agreed metrics, milestones and KPIs, producing high‑quality reports for senior leaders, Program Boards and the ART Executive Team
Lead and support internal and external assurance activities, including regulator engagement across the portfolio
Develop clear, concise governance papers and strategic reports for executive and board audiences, translating complex data into actionable insights
Drive continuous improvement by facilitating post‑project reviews and sharing lessons learned across delivery teams
Support change and communication activities to ensure stakeholders understand governance changes and impacts

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Proven experience in portfolio, program or project governance within large‑scale, technology‑driven transformation programs
Exposure to multiple delivery methodologies, including scaled agile environments
Strong understanding of governance frameworks and their practical application
Advanced analytical skills with the ability to identify trends and insights from complex data sets
Experience using portfolio and reporting tools such as Smartsheet, Jira, Excel and Power BI
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience producing executive‑level reporting
High attention to detail and a strong commitment to data quality and control
